analysis.overall_assessment: Your autonomic function shows concerning volatility with HRV dropping from 77ms to 35ms and resting HR spiking to 56 bpm on May 4th, suggesting significant sympathetic overdrive. Deep sleep remains suboptimal (averaging 1.2h vs. ideal 1.5-2h), which may be compromising both Hashimoto's immune regulation and vestibular compensation. Temperature stability at 0.0°C deviation suggests thyroid function is currently stable.
whats_going_well: Excellent sleep efficiency at 85.1% and consistent 7+ hour sleep duration shows your sleep protocol is working well
Temperature deviations remain stable at 0.0°C across the monitoring period, indicating well-controlled Hashimoto's without active immune flares
Activity levels are appropriately graded for POTS management, with steps ranging 3925-8365 and avoiding the overexertion crashes typical in dysautonomia
areas_to_improve: Deep sleep is critically low at 0.6-1.1h in recent days (optimal >1.5h) - consider increasing magnesium glycinate dose or moving dinner earlier to enhance sleep architecture
HRV volatility pattern shows 77ms dropping to 35ms with corresponding RHR spike to 56 bpm on May 4th - increase morning LMNT dose and add midday electrolyte support on high-stress days
The May 4th data shows classic POTS crash pattern (minimal sleep, elevated RHR, crashed HRV) - implement early warning system when HRV drops >20% from baseline to trigger rest protocol
analysis.autonomic_assessment: HRV averaging 63.6ms is decent but the 42ms swing from peak to crash (77→35ms) indicates unstable autonomic regulation typical of post-COVID POTS. The corresponding RHR spike from 44-47 bpm baseline to 56 bpm on crash day confirms sympathetic overdrive. Recovery metrics suggest your nervous system is operating near capacity with limited resilience.
analysis.thyroid_immune_signals: Temperature stability at 0.0°C deviation is excellent and suggests Hashimoto's is well-controlled with current selenium/zinc protocols. However, deep sleep deficiency (averaging 1.2h vs. optimal 1.5-2h) may compromise immune system repair and eventually trigger autoimmune activity.
analysis.vestibular_readiness: Readiness scores 77-89 indicate good capacity for vestibular rehab on most days, but the May 4th crash day should have prompted complete rest from balance training. HRV below 50ms signals insufficient nervous system capacity for compensation exercises.
analysis.focus_for_next_week: Prioritize deep sleep optimization through earlier dinner timing, increased magnesium glycinate to 600mg, and blue light blocking 2 hours before bed - your autonomic crashes correlate directly with poor sleep architecture.
